---
title: "nitrix.linalg.solve"
generated: true
signature-hash: eda0c68cbb071a4b
description: "Dense linear solves: general and SPD-Cholesky."
listing:
  id: nitrix-linalg-solve-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-linalg-solve/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.linalg.solve`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Dense linear solves: general and SPD-Cholesky.

The public surface for the small dense solves the rest of the library
implied but never exposed first-class.  [`cho_solve`](nitrix-linalg-solve/cho_solve.qmd) is the SPD
normal-equation solve behind the registration Gauss-Newton /
Levenberg-Marquardt step ($(J^{\top}J + \lambda I)\,\delta =
-J^{\top}r$) and any small symmetric-positive-definite system;
`solve` is the general (non-symmetric) $A x = b$.

Both batch over leading dimensions and are reverse-mode differentiable.
The right-hand side may be a vector `(..., n)` or a matrix
`(..., n, m)`; the trailing shape of the result matches.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
::: {.callout-note}
These delegate to the cuSolver-robust `safe_cho_solve` /
`safe_solve` wrappers, which route the factorisation to a device
where it works (CPU on stacks with the broken cuSolver handle pool) and
move the result back.  On a healthy GPU stack there is no overhead;
the triangular solve (cuBLAS) stays on-device.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-linalg-solve-symbols}
:::
